content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian and Bengali.Chef opened a company which manufactures cars and bikes. Each car requires 444 tyres while each bike requires 222 tyres. Chef has a total of NNN tyres (NNN is even). He wants to manufacture maximum number of cars from these tyres and then manufacture bikes from the remaining tyres.

Chef's friend went to Chef to purchase a bike. If Chef's company has manufactured even a single bike then Chef's friend will be able to purchase it.

Determine whether he will be able to purchase the bike or not.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN denoting the number of tyres.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` or `NO` depending on whether Chef's friend will be able to purchase the bike or not. Output is case insensitive.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single even integer N, the total number of tyres available for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if at least one bike can be manufactured after producing the maximum possible number of cars, otherwise output "NO". The output is case‑insensitive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: N=8, 8%4=0 → no bike → NO.\nTest case 2: N=2, 2%4=2 → one bike → YES.\nTest case 3: N=6, 6%4=2 → one bike after building one car → YES.
N=4 leaves 0 tyres after cars → NO. N=10 leaves 2 tyres after two cars → one bike → YES.
14%4=2, so after building three cars (12 tyres) two tyres remain for a bike.

solution.approach: For each N we first allocate as many cars as possible because each car consumes 4 tyres. The number of tyres left after building the maximum number of cars is N % 4. A bike needs exactly 2 tyres, therefore a bike can be built iff the remainder is at least 2. Since N is even, the remainder can only be 0 or 2, so the condition simplifies to (N % 4 == 2). Output YES when the condition holds, otherwise NO.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
